steps.title: Use corresponding sides
steps.content: Triangle $PQR$ has side lengths 2 times the corresponding side lengths of triangle $LMN$. Side $LM$ corresponds to side $PQ$.

steps.title: Identify corresponding sides
steps.content: Since $L$ corresponds to $P$, $M$ corresponds to $Q$, and $N$ corresponds to $R$, side $LM$ corresponds to side $PQ$.

steps.title: Apply the scale factor
steps.content:
Each side of triangle $PQR$ is 2 times the corresponding side in triangle $LMN$. Therefore:
$$PQ = 2 \cdot LM = 2 \cdot 18 = 36$$
That matches the correct answer - D is correct.
